mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days as investors price in higher future rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL > 235, or inflation expectations fail to translate into rate hike bets.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS > 140k and UNRATE < 5%, or individual proxies show no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with strong housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding supply-side capacity, creating deflationary pressures that drive S&P 500 returns negative over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO > 105 and HOUST > 1100, or individual proxies show no correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust economic demand, increasing corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through cash flow expansion.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 40% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2%, or GDP growth fails to correlate with equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% indicates labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, which drives S&P 500 returns positive over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 5%, or unemployment fails to predict equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a stimulative environment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through multiple expansion.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where RSAFS > 445k and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or individual proxies show no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) paired with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals a monetary tightening cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 235 and FEDFUNDS > 0.3%, or individual proxies show no correlation.
